violations.text: Ambient temperature of the 2-door lowboy unit observed at 43 degrees F. Cheese observed with a temperature of 42 degrees F. Temperature was adjusted and was observed at 36 degrees F at the end of inspection. In addition, the 1-door reach-in observed with an ambient temperature of 45 degrees F. Lemons in this unit observed with a temperature of 44 degrees F. TCS products (juice, cut fruit) were moved to properly functioning units during inspection. No TCS foods are to be stored in this unit until it is serviced and maintaining a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

The doors to the outside, located at the entrance, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine and shelving in the 1-door reach-in observed to have an accumulation of brown/black residue. These areas must be properly cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

Chili and sliced cheeses were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of black residue. New Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2023
